action_ref.content:
{
  "analysis": "Great! Pandas and PyArrow are already available. Now I'll create a Python script to examine the structure of the parquet files and convert them to fasttext format.",
  "plan": "I'll write a Python script to load the parquet files, examine their structure, and convert them to fasttext format (label followed by text). Then I'll run this script to prepare our training data.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > examine_data.py << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd

def examine_parquet(file_path):
    df = pd.read_parquet(file_path)
    print(f"File: {file_path}")
    print(f"Shape: {df.shape}")
    print("Columns:", df.columns.tolist())
    print("First few rows:")
    print(df.head())
    print("\nData types:")
    print(df.dtypes)
    return df

def convert_to_fasttext(df, text_col, label_col, output_file):
    with open(output_file, 'w') as f:
        for _, row in df.iterrows():
            # Format for fasttext: __label__<label> <text>
            line = f"__label__{row[label_col]} {row[text_col]}\n"
            f.write(line)
    print(f"Converted data saved to {output_file}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("Examining training data...")
    train_df = examine_parquet('train-00000-of-00001.parquet')
    
    print("\n\nExamining test data...")
    test_df = examine_parquet('test-00000-of-00001.parquet')
    
    # Assuming columns are 'text' and 'label', adjust as needed
    # Convert training data
    convert_to_fasttext(train_df, 'text', 'label', '../train.txt')
    
    # Convert test data
    convert_to_fasttext(test_df, 'text', 'label', '../test.txt')
EOF
",
      "duration": 1.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
